question: What should a customer do if they do not receive a password reset email?
answer:
If you do not receive a password reset email within five minutes, verify that you entered the correct email address. If your company uses a custom email domain, confirm that the AcmeTech notification email address (no-reply@acmetechsolutions.com) is not blocked by your organization's email security filters.

**Document IDs used:** SUP-001

Title: Password Reset Guide

Article: How to Reset Your AcmeTech Account Password
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
If you have forgotten your AcmeTech account password or are having trouble signing in, this article guides you through the steps to reset your password and regain access to your account. Password resets can typically be completed in under two minutes.

Self-Service Password Reset
To reset your password using the self-service flow, navigate to the AcmeTech login page and click the "Forgot password?" link below the password field. Enter the email address associated with your AcmeTech account and click "Send Reset Link." Check your inbox for an email from no-reply@acmetechsolutions.com. The email will arrive within two minutes in most cases; if you do not see it, please check your spam or junk folder. Click the reset link in the email. The link is valid for 30 minutes from the time it was sent. You will be directed to a page where you can create a new password. Your new password must be at least 12 characters long and must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. Confirm your new password and click "Reset Password." You will be redirected to the login page and can sign in with your new password immediately.

Common Troubleshooting Issues
If you did not receive the reset email within five minutes, verify that you entered the correct email address. If your company uses a custom email domain, confirm that the AcmeTech notification email address is not blocked by your organization's email security filters. If you are part of an organization that uses Single Sign-On (SSO) for AcmeTech access, you cannot reset your AcmeTech password directly. Contact your IT administrator to reset your credentials through your corporate identity provider.

Account Lockout
If your account has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts, you will need to wait 15 minutes before attempting to sign in again or request a password reset as described above. Accounts locked due to a suspected security event may require manual verification by the AcmeTech Support team. Please submit a support ticket if your account remains locked after a successful password reset.

Contacting Support
If you continue to experience issues accessing your account after completing the password reset, please contact the AcmeTech Customer Support team by submitting a ticket through the support portal at support.acmetechsolutions.com or by emailing support@acmetechsolutions.com. Please include your account email address and a description of the issue you are experiencing. ||| Document ID: SUP-002

Title: Account Recovery Guide

Article: Recovering Access to a Locked or Compromised AcmeTech Account
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: February 2026

Overview
This article explains how to recover access to your AcmeTech account in scenarios beyond a standard password reset, including locked accounts, suspected unauthorized access, or situations where you no longer have access to your registered email address.

Recovering a Locked Account
If your account is locked due to multiple failed login attempts, the lockout period is 15 minutes for standard login attempt failures. You may use the self-service password reset during the lockout period to immediately regain access with a new password. If your account is locked due to a security review triggered by suspicious activity, the lockout may remain in place until cleared by AcmeTech's Support team. In this case, submit a support ticket with subject line "Locked Account – Security Review" and include your account email, organization name, and a brief description of your recent account activity.

Suspected Unauthorized Access
If you believe your AcmeTech account has been accessed without your authorization, take the following steps immediately. Sign in to your account if possible and navigate to Account Settings > Active Sessions to view all currently active sessions. Revoke any sessions you do not recognize using the "Sign out" option next to each session. Change your password immediately using the settings available in Account Security. If you cannot sign in, use the self-service password reset. Enable multi-factor authentication if it is not already enabled. Contact AcmeTech Support to report the suspected breach. Our security team will investigate the account activity log and assist with containment.

Changing Your Registered Email Address
If you need to update the email address associated with your account, sign in and navigate to Account Settings > Profile. Enter your new email address and confirm by re-entering your current password. A verification email will be sent to the new address. Click the verification link to confirm the change. If you no longer have access to your original email address, contact Support with identity verification documentation before the change can be processed.

Organization Administrator Recovery
If you are an organization administrator who has lost access to your account and cannot use self-service recovery, AcmeTech requires multi-factor identity verification before restoring access. Contact Support via phone through your dedicated account number or email support with a formal written request on company letterhead. Processing time for administrator account recovery requests is one to two business days. ||| Document ID: SUP-004

Title: Billing Issues Troubleshooting Guide

Article: Resolving Billing Issues with Your AcmeTech Account
Category: Billing and Subscriptions
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
This article addresses common billing issues that AcmeTech customers may encounter, including failed payment processing, unexpected charges, invoice discrepancies, and subscription cancellations. For urgent billing issues that are preventing access to your account, contact the AcmeTech Billing Support team immediately.

Failed Payment Processing
If a payment for your AcmeTech subscription has failed, you will receive an email notification from billing@acmetechsolutions.com. Your account will remain active for seven days following a failed payment to allow time to resolve the issue. To resolve a failed payment, sign in and navigate to Billing > Payment Methods. Verify that your payment method details are current, including the card number, expiration date, and billing address. Update the payment method if necessary and click "Retry Payment." If the payment continues to fail, contact your card issuer or bank to confirm that there are no restrictions on transactions to AcmeTech.

Unexpected Charges
If you see a charge from AcmeTech that you do not recognize, check your account billing history in the Billing section of Account Settings for a detailed breakdown of all charges. Common reasons for unexpected charges include mid-cycle plan upgrades, additional user seat purchases, and overage charges for usage-based features. If you are unable to identify the source of a charge after reviewing your billing history, contact the AcmeTech Billing team with the charge date and amount and a team member will investigate within two business days.

Invoice Discrepancies
If your invoice does not match your expected subscription pricing, verify that your plan tier and any discounts or promotional pricing are reflected correctly in your Account Settings. Invoices can be downloaded as PDFs from the Billing History section. If the discrepancy persists, submit a support ticket with the invoice number and a description of the expected versus actual amounts.

Requesting a Refund
AcmeTech's standard policy is that subscription fees are non-refundable except in cases of verified billing errors or circumstances covered by AcmeTech's 30-day satisfaction guarantee for new subscriptions. Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the charge date. Contact the Billing team with your account information and the reason for the refund request.

Account Suspension
If your account has been suspended due to non-payment, access to the AcmeTech platform will be restricted until the overdue balance is paid. Contact the Billing team to arrange payment and request reactivation. Account data is retained for 90 days following suspension before it is subject to deletion. ||| Document ID: SUP-008

Title: Mobile App Troubleshooting Guide

Article: Troubleshooting AcmeTech Mobile App Issues
Category: Mobile App
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
This article covers the most common issues experienced by users of the AcmeTech mobile app on iOS and Android devices, along with step-by-step troubleshooting instructions. If your issue is not covered here, please submit a support ticket through the app or the support portal.

Cannot Sign In to the Mobile App
If you are unable to sign in to the AcmeTech mobile app, try the following steps. Confirm you are using the correct email address and password associated with your AcmeTech account. If you have forgotten your password, use the "Forgot password?" link on the login screen. Verify that your device has an active internet connection by opening another app that requires connectivity. If your organization uses Single Sign-On, tap "Sign in with SSO" and enter your organization's SSO domain rather than your password. Ensure your device's operating system is up to date; the AcmeTech app requires iOS 16 or higher and Android 12 or higher. If the issue persists, uninstall and reinstall the app from the App Store or Google Play.

App Crashes or Freezes
If the AcmeTech mobile app crashes or becomes unresponsive, force-close the app and reopen it. If the app crashes consistently on launch, uninstall it and reinstall the latest version from the app store. Check that your device has at least 200 MB of available storage. Clearing the app's cache on Android devices (Settings > Apps > AcmeTech > Storage > Clear Cache) can resolve many performance-related issues. If crashes occur in a specific feature or after a recent app update, submit a support ticket through the support portal and include your device model, OS version, and a description of the steps that reproduce the crash.

Notifications Not Arriving
If you are not receiving push notifications from the AcmeTech mobile app, check that notification permissions are enabled for AcmeTech in your device settings. On iOS, navigate to Settings > AcmeTech > Notifications and ensure "Allow Notifications" is enabled. On Android, navigate to Settings > Apps > AcmeTech > Notifications. If notifications are enabled at the device level, check in-app notification preferences by navigating to Profile > Notification Settings within the AcmeTech app.

Data Not Syncing
If data in the mobile app appears out of date, pull down on the main screen to force a manual refresh. Check your device's network connectivity. If you are connected to Wi-Fi, try switching to your mobile data connection to rule out a Wi-Fi-specific issue. Sign out and sign back in to the app to force a full data refresh. If a specific record or document is not syncing, check whether the same data is accessible via the web platform to determine whether the issue is app-specific or account-specific.

answer:

- Employees at the **Senior Manager level and above**

New employees in eligible roles must complete **a minimum of 90 days of service** before a card is issued.

Title: Corporate Credit Card Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Corporate Credit Card Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Accounting

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions issues corporate credit cards to employees who regularly incur business expenses as part of their role. This policy governs the issuance, use, and management of corporate credit cards to ensure proper financial controls, accurate expense tracking, and compliance with AcmeTech's budgetary standards.

Card Issuance Eligibility
Corporate credit cards are issued to employees at the Senior Manager level and above, employees in roles that require frequent business travel, and other employees as approved by the CFO based on documented business need. New employees in eligible roles must complete a minimum of 90 days of service before a card is issued. Card requests must be submitted by the employee's manager to the Finance team with a written business justification.

Authorized Use
Corporate credit cards are to be used solely for legitimate AcmeTech business expenses. Authorized categories include business travel, client meals and entertainment with prior manager approval, approved procurement purchases, and other pre-approved business expenditures. Under no circumstances may corporate cards be used for personal purchases, cash advances, or any expenditure outside the scope of approved business activities.

Expense Reconciliation
Cardholders are required to reconcile all corporate card transactions within 10 business days of each month-end statement date. Reconciliation must be completed through the expense management system, with receipts attached for each transaction. Transactions that cannot be reconciled with a valid business justification will be charged back to the cardholder through payroll deduction.

Reporting Lost or Stolen Cards
Cardholders must immediately report a lost or stolen corporate card to the Finance team and to the card issuer's 24-hour hotline. AcmeTech will not be responsible for unauthorized charges incurred after the card was reported missing. The Finance team will issue a replacement card within five business days of the report.

Card Suspension and Termination
Corporate cards will be immediately suspended upon an employee's termination of employment. Managers are responsible for ensuring cards are deactivated for employees who transfer to roles where card usage is no longer required. Repeated policy violations, including personal use of the card, failure to reconcile, or late submissions, will result in card revocation and potential disciplinary action. ||| Document ID: SUP-014

Title: Onboarding New Team Members Guide

Article: Onboarding New Team Members to AcmeTech
Category: Administration
Last Updated: February 2026

Overview
This article guides organization administrators through the steps to successfully onboard new team members to AcmeTech, from initial account invitation through first-day setup and training resources.

Step 1: Invite the New User
Navigate to Account Settings > Users and click "Invite User." Enter the new team member's work email address and select the appropriate role for their responsibilities. If your organization uses SSO, the new user will be provisioned automatically when they first sign in using your corporate identity provider; a manual invitation may not be required depending on your SSO configuration. If you are using AcmeTech's native authentication, the invitation email will be sent from noreply@acmetechsolutions.com. The invitation expires after 72 hours; you can resend it from the Users section if needed.

Step 2: Assign Groups and Permissions
If your organization uses custom groups to manage access to specific projects, customers, or features, add the new team member to the appropriate groups before their first login. Navigate to Account Settings > Groups, select the relevant group, and add the new user. Group membership determines which data and features the user can access within their assigned role.

Step 3: Share Getting Started Resources
AcmeTech provides a Getting Started guide and video tutorial series accessible from the Help Center at help.acmetechsolutions.com. Share the following resources with new team members: the Getting Started Guide for their specific role (Member, Admin, or View Only), the AcmeTech Quick Start video (15 minutes), and the keyboard shortcuts reference card. The in-app onboarding checklist will appear automatically for new users on their first login and guides them through the platform's core features.

Step 4: Schedule a Walkthrough
For teams onboarding five or more new users at once, AcmeTech offers a complimentary onboarding webinar facilitated by a Customer Success team member. Professional and Enterprise plan customers can schedule an onboarding session by contacting their Customer Success Manager or emailing onboarding@acmetechsolutions.com. The session covers platform navigation, key workflows, and best practices relevant to your team's use case.

Step 5: Monitor Adoption
After onboarding, track new user adoption through the User Activity Report in Account Settings > Users > Reports. Users who have not completed the in-app onboarding checklist or who have not logged in within seven days of invitation can be identified in this report. Follow up with these users to address any barriers to adoption. AcmeTech's Customer Success team can provide additional enablement resources on request. ||| Document ID: EMP-010

Title: Bonus Calculation Framework

AcmeTech Solutions Bonus Calculation Framework
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Human Resources

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions offers performance-based variable compensation in the form of annual bonuses to eligible employees. This Bonus Calculation Framework describes the structure, eligibility criteria, performance metrics, and payout mechanics of AcmeTech's annual bonus program.

Eligibility
All full-time permanent employees who are actively employed on December 31 of the performance year and who have completed a minimum of six months of continuous service are eligible for the annual bonus. Employees on unpaid leave for more than 90 days during the performance year will have their bonus calculated on a prorated basis reflecting actual days worked. Employees who resign or are terminated before December 31 are not eligible for the annual bonus unless explicitly stated in their employment agreement.

Target Bonus Levels
Bonus targets are expressed as a percentage of annual base salary and vary by job level. Level 1–3 employees have a target of 5% of base salary. Level 4–5 employees have a target of 10%. Level 6–7 (Manager and Director) have a target of 15–20%. Level 8 (VP) has a target of 25–30%. C-suite executives have individual targets defined in their employment agreements.

Bonus Calculation
The actual bonus payout is calculated as: Target Bonus x Company Performance Multiplier x Individual Performance Multiplier. The Company Performance Multiplier is set by the executive team and board based on company-wide revenue, EBITDA, and strategic goal achievement against the annual plan. The multiplier ranges from 0 to 1.5. The Individual Performance Multiplier is derived from the employee's annual performance rating: Outstanding (1.25), Exceeds Expectations (1.10), Meets Expectations (1.0), Needs Improvement (0.5), Unsatisfactory (0).

Payout Timing
Annual bonuses are paid in the February payroll cycle of the year following the performance year. Bonuses are subject to standard income tax withholding. Employees who joined the company during the performance year will receive a prorated bonus based on their start date.

Discretionary Nature
AcmeTech reserves the right to modify, reduce, or eliminate the bonus program in any year based on business conditions, at the sole discretion of the executive team and board. Participation in the bonus program does not constitute a guarantee of any specific payout amount or the continuation of the program in future years.

question: How is an employee's actual annual bonus payout calculated?
answer:
The actual bonus payout is calculated as:
**Target Bonus × Company Performance Multiplier × Individual Performance Multiplier**.

- **Target Bonus**: A percentage of annual base salary (e.g., 5% for Level 1–3 employees, 10% for Level 4–5, etc.).
- **Company Performance Multiplier**: Set by the executive team and board (ranges from 0 to 1.5).
- **Individual Performance Multiplier**: Based on the employee's annual performance rating:
  - Outstanding: 1.25
  - Exceeds Expectations: 1.10
  - Meets Expectations: 1.0
  - Needs Improvement: 0.5
  - Unsatisfactory: 0

Title: Employee Referral Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Employee Referral Program Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations and Talent Acquisition

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions values employee referrals as one of the most effective channels for attracting high-quality talent. This Employee Referral Policy outlines the referral bonus program, eligibility requirements, and the process for submitting and tracking referrals.

Eligibility
All permanent full-time and part-time employees are eligible to participate in the referral program. HR employees directly involved in the hiring process for a specific role are not eligible to receive a referral bonus for that role. Contractors, interns, and employees on active Performance Improvement Plans are not eligible to participate.

Referral Process
Employees wishing to refer a candidate must submit the referral through the AcmeTech internal careers portal before the candidate applies independently. Late referrals submitted after the candidate has already applied will not be eligible for a bonus. The referral submission must include the candidate's full name, contact information, and the position being referred for.

Referral Bonus Structure
Referrals that result in a successful hire are eligible for a referral bonus paid to the referring employee. The bonus amount is $2,500 for non-technical roles and $4,000 for technical roles at the Engineer, Senior Engineer, and Product Manager level. For Director-level and above positions, the referral bonus is $7,500. Bonuses are paid in two installments: 50% upon the referred candidate's hire date and 50% upon the referred candidate's successful completion of the 90-day probationary period.

Conditions
The referral bonus is not payable if the referring employee has left AcmeTech before either installment date. Both the referring employee and the referred candidate must be employed at AcmeTech at the time of each installment payment. Referral bonuses are subject to applicable tax withholding and will be included in the referring employee's paycheck.

Program Integrity
AcmeTech reserves the right to modify, suspend, or terminate the referral program at any time. Submission of false referrals or manipulation of the program may result in disciplinary action. ||| Document ID: HR-001
Category: HR Policies
Title: Annual Leave Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Annual Leave Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions is committed to providing employees with adequate time away from work to rest, recharge, and attend to personal matters. This Annual Leave Policy outlines employee entitlements, accrual methods, approval procedures, and compliance expectations applicable to all full-time and part-time employees.

Leave Entitlements
All full-time employees are entitled to a minimum of 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year, accruing at a rate of 1.67 days per month. Employees with more than five years of continuous service receive an additional five days, bringing total entitlement to 25 days. Part-time employees receive leave on a pro-rated basis proportional to their contracted hours. Employees in their probationary period may accrue leave but may not take leave until the probationary period is successfully completed, unless approved by the HR department under exceptional circumstances.

Carryover Rules
A maximum of five unused annual leave days may be carried over from one calendar year to the next. Any accrued leave in excess of five days at year-end will be forfeited unless an employee has an active approved leave request that could not be fulfilled due to business requirements. Employees must request carryover extensions in writing to their HR Business Partner no later than November 30.

Leave Request and Approval Process
Employees must submit leave requests through the AcmeTech HR self-service portal with a minimum of five business days notice for absences of one to three days, and a minimum of ten business days notice for absences of four or more consecutive days. Requests are subject to manager approval, which should be granted or declined within three business days of submission. Managers are responsible for ensuring adequate team coverage during leave periods. Denial of a leave request must be accompanied by a written explanation and a proposed alternative leave window.

Leave During Notice Period
Employees who have resigned or been issued notice of termination may take approved annual leave during the notice period at the manager's discretion. In cases where leave cannot be accommodated, accrued leave balances will be paid out in the final paycheck in accordance with applicable employment law.

Policy Compliance
Failure to obtain leave approval before absence, falsification of leave records, or abuse of the leave policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Employees with concerns about leave entitlements or approvals should contact the HR department at hrteam@acmetechsolutions.com. ||| Document ID: FIN-012

Title: Equity Compensation Guide

AcmeTech Solutions Equity Compensation Guide
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Human Resources

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions offers equity compensation as a component of total compensation for eligible employees. This guide provides an overview of AcmeTech's equity plan, including grant types, vesting schedules, exercise processes, and tax implications.

Equity Plan Overview
AcmeTech grants equity in the form of Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) to all full-time employees at the Senior level and above. Stock options may also be granted to certain senior and executive employees as specified in their offer letters or annual equity review. RSU grants are made annually in February as part of the compensation review cycle.

Vesting Schedule
Standard RSU grants vest over a four-year period with a one-year cliff. Employees must be continuously employed at AcmeTech on each vesting date to receive the vested shares. Acceleration of vesting may occur in certain scenarios, including a change of control event as defined in the equity plan documents.

Tax Treatment of RSUs
RSUs are taxable as ordinary income at the time of vesting, based on the fair market value of the shares on the vesting date. AcmeTech withholds the applicable payroll taxes at vesting, typically by retaining a portion of the vested shares to cover the tax obligation through net share settlement. Employees are responsible for reporting RSU income on their personal tax returns. The Finance team coordinates with the payroll system to ensure RSU tax events are reflected accurately in W-2 reporting.

Equity Grants for New Hires
New hire RSU grants are made upon the first regularly scheduled grant cycle following the employee's 90-day probationary period. Grant sizes are determined based on job level and are reviewed annually against market benchmarks by the Finance and HR teams. Grant amounts are communicated to employees through their offer letter and confirmed in a formal grant agreement issued through the equity management platform.

Termination and Equity
Unvested RSUs are forfeited upon termination of employment for any reason. Employees who resign voluntarily or are terminated for cause forfeit all unvested equity. In cases of termination without cause, the Finance and HR teams will review any accelerated vesting provisions in the employee's specific agreement before finalizing equity outcomes.

question: What is the default API rate limit for a Professional plan customer, and what happens when the limit is exceeded?
answer:
The default API rate limit for a **Professional Plan** customer is **1,000 requests per minute**.

When the limit is exceeded, the AcmeTech API returns an **HTTP 429 Too Many Requests** response, including a **Retry-After** header indicating the number of seconds to wait before retrying. You should implement **exponential backoff with jitter** when encountering this error.

**Sources:**

Title: API Rate Limits Guide

Article: Understanding AcmeTech API Rate Limits
Category: API and Integrations
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
AcmeTech enforces API rate limits to ensure fair usage of the platform and to protect system stability for all customers. This article explains how rate limits work, how to check your current usage, how to handle rate limit errors, and how to request a rate limit increase.

Default Rate Limits
AcmeTech API rate limits are applied per customer organization. The default rate limits by plan tier are: Starter Plan: 500 requests per minute. Professional Plan: 1,000 requests per minute. Enterprise Plan: Customizable, with a default of 5,000 requests per minute configurable up to your contracted maximum. Rate limits apply to all API endpoints collectively. Specific high-throughput endpoints such as batch operations may have additional per-endpoint limits documented in the API reference.

How Rate Limiting Works
AcmeTech uses a sliding window rate limiting algorithm. Your request quota is replenished continuously rather than resetting at a fixed interval. This means that if you send 500 requests in the first 10 seconds of a minute, you are not allowed to send 500 more requests at the start of the next minute; instead, you regain capacity gradually as older requests move out of the sliding window.

Handling Rate Limit Errors
When your request rate exceeds the configured limit, the AcmeTech API returns an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests response. The response includes a Retry-After header indicating the number of seconds you should wait before retrying. Your integration should implement exponential backoff with jitter when encountering 429 responses. Do not immediately retry at full request volume after the Retry-After period; increase the delay progressively if rate limit errors persist. Monitoring your API usage against your rate limits proactively is the best way to avoid unexpected 429 errors in production.

Monitoring API Usage
Organization administrators can view real-time and historical API usage in the AcmeTech developer dashboard under Settings > API Usage. Usage data is updated every minute. Alerts can be configured to notify your team when API usage exceeds a specified percentage of your rate limit. Setting up usage alerts is strongly recommended for all integrations with high request volumes.

Requesting a Rate Limit Increase
Enterprise customers who require higher rate limits than their default allocation can request an increase through their dedicated account manager or by submitting a support ticket with the subject "Rate Limit Increase Request." Include your organization name, current limit, requested limit, and a brief description of your use case. Rate limit increases for Enterprise customers are typically approved within two business days. ||| Document ID: SUP-003

Title: Subscription Upgrade Guide

Article: How to Upgrade Your AcmeTech Subscription Plan
Category: Billing and Subscriptions
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
This article explains how to upgrade your AcmeTech subscription to a higher plan tier, what to expect during the upgrade process, and how billing is handled for mid-cycle upgrades.

Available Subscription Plans
AcmeTech offers three subscription tiers. The Starter plan is designed for small teams and includes core platform access for up to 10 users with standard support. The Professional plan is designed for growing teams and includes advanced features, up to 50 users, and priority support. The Enterprise plan is designed for large organizations and includes full feature access, unlimited users, custom integrations, dedicated account management, and enterprise SLA support.

How to Upgrade
To upgrade your subscription, sign in to your AcmeTech account as an organization administrator and navigate to the Billing section in your Account Settings. Click "Change Plan" and select the plan you wish to upgrade to. Review the plan features and pricing summary on the plan comparison page. Click "Confirm Upgrade" to proceed. If your account is on a monthly billing cycle, the new plan pricing takes effect immediately and your first invoice at the new rate will be generated at the next billing date. If your account is on an annual billing cycle, you will be charged a prorated amount for the remainder of the current annual term at the time of the upgrade. Your upgraded plan features will be available immediately upon confirmation.

User Seat Adjustments
When upgrading to a plan with a higher user limit, additional user seats become available immediately. If your current user count exceeds the limit of your new plan, you will be prompted to remove users before the upgrade can be confirmed. AcmeTech does not automatically remove users; an administrator must manage user access through the User Management section.

Enterprise Plan Upgrades
Upgrades to the Enterprise plan cannot be completed through the self-service billing portal. Contact your AcmeTech account manager or reach out to the sales team at sales@acmetechsolutions.com to discuss your Enterprise plan requirements. The Enterprise onboarding process typically takes three to five business days and includes a kickoff call with your dedicated account team.

Billing Inquiries
For questions about subscription pricing, invoice details, or billing adjustments, contact the AcmeTech Billing team via the support portal at support.acmetechsolutions.com or call the billing hotline available on your account contact page. ||| Document ID: HR-010
Category: HR Policies
Title: Health Insurance Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Employee Health Insurance Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations and Benefits Administration

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions provides comprehensive health insurance coverage to all eligible full-time employees as part of its total compensation package. This document outlines the insurance plans available, employee contribution requirements, enrollment procedures, and benefit coverage details.

Eligible Employees
All full-time employees working 30 or more hours per week are eligible for health insurance coverage beginning on the first day of the month following their hire date. Employees may also enroll eligible dependents, including legal spouses, domestic partners, and dependent children up to the age of 26.

Available Plans
AcmeTech offers three plan tiers through its insurance carrier partnership: the Standard Plan, the Enhanced Plan, and the Premium Plan. The Standard Plan covers 80% of in-network medical costs after a $1,500 annual deductible. The Enhanced Plan covers 90% of in-network medical costs after a $750 deductible and includes dental and vision coverage. The Premium Plan covers 100% of in-network medical costs after a $250 deductible and includes enhanced dental, vision, and mental health benefits.

Employee Contributions
AcmeTech contributes 80% of the monthly premium for the Standard Plan for all eligible employees. Employee contributions for the Enhanced and Premium Plans vary based on coverage tier and are deducted from bi-weekly paychecks on a pre-tax basis. Dependent coverage is available at an additional cost to the employee.

Enrollment and Changes
Open enrollment takes place each year during the month of October, with coverage changes effective January 1. Outside of open enrollment, employees may make changes to their coverage within 30 days of a qualifying life event, including marriage, divorce, birth or adoption of a child, or loss of coverage under another plan. Enrollment changes must be submitted through the HR benefits portal.

Mental Health and Wellness Benefits
All health insurance plans include coverage for mental health services, including therapy, psychiatric consultations, and substance use treatment. AcmeTech also provides all employees with access to the HealthMind employee assistance program, which offers confidential counseling services, financial wellness support, and well-being resources at no cost. ||| Document ID: FIN-011
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Revenue Recognition Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Revenue Recognition Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions recognizes revenue in accordance with ASC 606, Revenue from Contracts with Customers. This policy ensures that revenue is recorded accurately, consistently, and in compliance with applicable accounting standards. All revenue-related transactions must be reviewed by Finance before recognition.

Five-Step Model
AcmeTech applies the ASC 606 five-step model to all customer contracts. Step 1: Identify the contract with the customer. Step 2: Identify the performance obligations in the contract. Step 3: Determine the transaction price. Step 4: Allocate the transaction price to each performance obligation. Step 5: Recognize revenue when or as each performance obligation is satisfied.

SaaS Subscription Revenue
Subscription revenue is recognized ratably over the contract term, beginning on the date the customer's access to the platform is provisioned. Annual and multi-year subscription payments received in advance are recorded as deferred revenue and recognized proportionally on a monthly basis. Any discounts or variable consideration in subscription contracts must be evaluated and documented by Finance before contract execution.

Professional Services Revenue
Revenue from implementation and consulting services is recognized as services are delivered, based on the percentage of completion method. Project milestones used for revenue recognition must be documented in the statement of work and approved by Finance prior to contract signing. Revenue from time-and-materials engagements is recognized monthly based on actual hours delivered.

Contract Modifications
Any changes to existing customer contracts that affect the scope, price, or term must be reviewed by Finance before execution. Modifications may require revenue to be reallocated among performance obligations or may be treated as a separate contract depending on the nature of the change. Sales and Legal must engage Finance on all material contract modifications.

Title: Probationary Period Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Probationary Period Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions uses a probationary period for all new employees to allow both the employee and the organization to assess fit and performance before confirming permanent employment. This policy outlines the duration, expectations, and outcomes of the probationary period.

Duration
The standard probationary period is 90 days from the employee's start date. For senior leadership roles at Director level and above, the probationary period is extended to 180 days. The probationary period may be extended for an additional 30 days by mutual agreement between the manager and HR if additional time is needed to assess performance.

Expectations During Probation
During the probationary period, new employees are expected to complete all required onboarding activities, demonstrate the competencies and behaviors required for their role, and meet any specific performance milestones established in their onboarding plan. Managers are expected to provide structured feedback at 30, 60, and 90 days through formal check-in meetings documented in the HR system.

Leave During Probation
Employees in their probationary period accrue annual leave but may not take leave without specific HR approval. Sick leave may be taken as required. Any absence of five or more consecutive days may result in the probationary period being extended by the length of the absence.

Completion of Probation
Upon successful completion of the probationary period, the employee's status is confirmed as permanent. The manager will communicate this formally in a written confirmation letter sent through the HR portal. Salary reviews associated with successful completion will be processed in the next available payroll cycle.

Termination During Probation
Employment may be terminated during the probationary period with one week's written notice if performance or conduct does not meet expectations. Termination during probation does not require progression through the standard disciplinary process, though HR must be consulted before any termination decision is made. ||| Document ID: HR-001
Category: HR Policies
Title: Annual Leave Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Annual Leave Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions is committed to providing employees with adequate time away from work to rest, recharge, and attend to personal matters. This Annual Leave Policy outlines employee entitlements, accrual methods, approval procedures, and compliance expectations applicable to all full-time and part-time employees.

Leave Entitlements
All full-time employees are entitled to a minimum of 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year, accruing at a rate of 1.67 days per month. Employees with more than five years of continuous service receive an additional five days, bringing total entitlement to 25 days. Part-time employees receive leave on a pro-rated basis proportional to their contracted hours. Employees in their probationary period may accrue leave but may not take leave until the probationary period is successfully completed, unless approved by the HR department under exceptional circumstances.

Carryover Rules
A maximum of five unused annual leave days may be carried over from one calendar year to the next. Any accrued leave in excess of five days at year-end will be forfeited unless an employee has an active approved leave request that could not be fulfilled due to business requirements. Employees must request carryover extensions in writing to their HR Business Partner no later than November 30.

Leave Request and Approval Process
Employees must submit leave requests through the AcmeTech HR self-service portal with a minimum of five business days notice for absences of one to three days, and a minimum of ten business days notice for absences of four or more consecutive days. Requests are subject to manager approval, which should be granted or declined within three business days of submission. Managers are responsible for ensuring adequate team coverage during leave periods. Denial of a leave request must be accompanied by a written explanation and a proposed alternative leave window.

Leave During Notice Period
Employees who have resigned or been issued notice of termination may take approved annual leave during the notice period at the manager's discretion. In cases where leave cannot be accommodated, accrued leave balances will be paid out in the final paycheck in accordance with applicable employment law.

Policy Compliance
Failure to obtain leave approval before absence, falsification of leave records, or abuse of the leave policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Employees with concerns about leave entitlements or approvals should contact the HR department at hrteam@acmetechsolutions.com. ||| Document ID: FIN-005
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Bonus Calculation Framework

AcmeTech Solutions Bonus Calculation Framework
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Human Resources

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions offers performance-based variable compensation in the form of annual bonuses to eligible employees. This Bonus Calculation Framework describes the structure, eligibility criteria, performance metrics, and payout mechanics of AcmeTech's annual bonus program.

Eligibility
All full-time permanent employees who are actively employed on December 31 of the performance year and who have completed a minimum of six months of continuous service are eligible for the annual bonus. Employees on unpaid leave for more than 90 days during the performance year will have their bonus calculated on a prorated basis reflecting actual days worked. Employees who resign or are terminated before December 31 are not eligible for the annual bonus unless explicitly stated in their employment agreement.

Target Bonus Levels
Bonus targets are expressed as a percentage of annual base salary and vary by job level. Level 1–3 employees have a target of 5% of base salary. Level 4–5 employees have a target of 10%. Level 6–7 (Manager and Director) have a target of 15–20%. Level 8 (VP) has a target of 25–30%. C-suite executives have individual targets defined in their employment agreements.

Bonus Calculation
The actual bonus payout is calculated as: Target Bonus x Company Performance Multiplier x Individual Performance Multiplier. The Company Performance Multiplier is set by the executive team and board based on company-wide revenue, EBITDA, and strategic goal achievement against the annual plan. The multiplier ranges from 0 to 1.5. The Individual Performance Multiplier is derived from the employee's annual performance rating: Outstanding (1.25), Exceeds Expectations (1.10), Meets Expectations (1.0), Needs Improvement (0.5), Unsatisfactory (0).

Payout Timing
Annual bonuses are paid in the February payroll cycle of the year following the performance year. Bonuses are subject to standard income tax withholding. Employees who joined the company during the performance year will receive a prorated bonus based on their start date.

Discretionary Nature
AcmeTech reserves the right to modify, reduce, or eliminate the bonus program in any year based on business conditions, at the sole discretion of the executive team and board. Participation in the bonus program does not constitute a guarantee of any specific payout amount or the continuation of the program in future years. ||| Document ID: HR-008
Category: HR Policies
Title: Internal Mobility Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Internal Mobility Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions is committed to supporting the career growth of its employees by fostering a culture of internal mobility. This policy encourages employees to explore career opportunities within the organization, facilitating movement across departments, roles, and locations where it serves both the employee's career goals and business needs.

Eligibility
Employees must have completed a minimum of 12 months in their current role before applying for an internal transfer. Employees on a Performance Improvement Plan, under formal disciplinary review, or in their probationary period are not eligible for internal mobility until their situation is resolved. Exceptions may be granted by the Chief People Officer in rare circumstances.

Application Process
Eligible employees may view all open internal positions on AcmeTech's internal careers portal. Employees should notify their current manager before submitting a formal application to ensure transparency, though this notification is not a prerequisite. Applications must be submitted through the internal portal along with a cover letter and updated skills profile. HR will acknowledge receipt within three business days.

Interview and Selection
Internal candidates will go through the same interview and assessment process as external candidates. Hiring managers may not discriminate against or give preferential treatment to internal candidates on the basis of their current department or team. All internal candidates will receive interview feedback within five business days of the final interview.

Transition Period
Employees accepted for internal transfers must provide their current team with a minimum of four weeks transition notice. The outgoing manager and the hiring manager will agree on a handover plan to minimize disruption. Compensation changes associated with the new role will take effect on the first day in the new position.

Career Development Support
The HR team and hiring manager will work with the newly transferred employee to establish a 90-day onboarding plan for the new role. The employee's career development goals will be revisited and updated in the HR system within 30 days of the transfer. AcmeTech provides financial support for any training or certification needed to support the transition.

Title: Paternity Leave Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Paternity Leave Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions values the role of all parents in nurturing their children and supports a culture where employees can take meaningful time away from work following the birth or adoption of a child. This Paternity Leave Policy outlines the entitlements and procedures for employees who are the secondary caregiver following the arrival of a new child.

Leave Entitlement
All eligible employees who are the secondary caregiver are entitled to four weeks of fully paid paternity leave, to be taken within six months of the birth or adoption placement date. Employees may take leave in one continuous block or in two separate blocks of a minimum of one week each, subject to manager approval. Eligibility requires completion of the probationary period prior to the expected birth or adoption date.

Additional Unpaid Leave
Employees may request up to an additional four weeks of unpaid leave following the paid paternity entitlement period. Unpaid leave requests must be submitted to HR and manager in writing a minimum of four weeks in advance. Approval will be granted based on business needs and staffing requirements.

Notification Requirements
Employees should notify their manager and HR Business Partner at the earliest practicable time after becoming aware of the expected birth or adoption, and no later than six weeks before the intended leave start date. A formal paternity leave request form must be submitted through the HR self-service portal.

Benefits During Leave
Full health insurance benefits, including medical, dental, and vision, will be maintained during the paid paternity leave period. Annual leave continues to accrue during paid leave. Retirement contributions will also continue in line with company policy during paid leave periods.

Return to Work and Role Protection
Employees are guaranteed their same role or an equivalent role upon return from paternity leave. AcmeTech strongly encourages managers to maintain regular, non-pressuring contact with employees on leave to support a smooth reintegration. Managers must not make adverse employment decisions based on an employee's use of paternity leave entitlements. Any concerns about role protection should be directed to the HR department immediately. ||| Document ID: FIN-009
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Budget Planning Guidelines

AcmeTech Solutions Annual Budget Planning Guidelines
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance

Policy Statement
This document provides department leaders and finance business partners with guidelines for the annual budget planning process at AcmeTech Solutions. The budget plan is a critical financial management tool that aligns resource allocation with company strategy and ensures accountability for financial performance throughout the year.

Budget Planning Timeline
The annual budget planning process begins in October each year for the following fiscal year. October is designated for top-down guidance distribution, including revenue targets, headcount limits, and strategic investment priorities. November is reserved for department-level budget submissions. December is used for Finance consolidation, leadership review, and iteration. Budget approval by the board takes place in the first week of January.

Budget Submission Requirements
Each department head must submit a budget proposal covering the following categories: personnel costs including base salary, benefits, and employer taxes; direct operating costs relevant to the department's function; technology and software costs; travel and entertainment; professional services and consulting; and capital expenditures if applicable. All submissions must be made using the standard budget template provided by Finance and uploaded to the budgeting platform by November 15.

Headcount Planning
Headcount plans must be submitted alongside financial budget submissions. Headcount plans should include current approved headcount, proposed new hires by role and expected start date, planned promotions, and anticipated attrition. All net new headcount above the approved baseline requires documented business justification and must be included in a formal headcount request reviewed by the CFO and CPO.

Budget Assumptions and Sensitivities
Department heads should document the key assumptions underlying their budget submissions, including expected business volume, pricing changes, or operational efficiencies. Finance will provide a set of company-wide macro assumptions including inflation rates, currency exchange rates, and headcount cost factors to ensure consistency across submissions.

In-Year Budget Management
Approved budgets are tracked monthly through the expense management and financial reporting system. Budget versus actuals reports are distributed to department heads on the fifth business day after each month close. Requests to transfer budget between categories or to access unplanned spending require Finance Director approval through the budget amendment process.

Document ID: SUP-007
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Security Settings Guide

Article: Configuring Security Settings for Your AcmeTech Organization
Category: Security and Compliance
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
AcmeTech provides a comprehensive set of security settings that organization administrators can configure to meet their organization's security and compliance requirements. This article covers multi-factor authentication enforcement, session management, IP allowlisting, and audit log access.

Enabling Organization-Wide Multi-Factor Authentication
Organization administrators can enforce MFA for all users in the organization by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Authentication. Toggle the "Require Multi-Factor Authentication" setting to enabled. Once enabled, all users who have not already configured MFA will be required to set up an authenticator app at their next login before they can access the platform. Users who have already configured MFA are unaffected. AcmeTech supports TOTP-based authenticator apps and WebAuthn hardware security keys for MFA.

Session Management Settings
Administrators can configure the session timeout duration and the maximum session length for their organization. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > Sessions. The session inactivity timeout can be set between 15 minutes and 24 hours. The absolute maximum session duration can be set between 1 hour and 30 days. Sessions that exceed either threshold are automatically terminated, requiring users to sign in again. Administrators can view and revoke active sessions for any user in their organization from the User Management section.

IP Allowlisting
Enterprise plan customers can restrict AcmeTech access to specific IP address ranges by configuring an IP allowlist. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > IP Allowlist and enter the authorized IP address ranges in CIDR notation. When an allowlist is active, access attempts from IP addresses outside the configured ranges will be blocked with an access denied error. Test your allowlist configuration carefully before enabling it to ensure that all legitimate users are included. The allowlist can be disabled immediately if users are inadvertently locked out.

Audit Log Access
AcmeTech maintains a comprehensive audit log of all administrative actions, user login events, permission changes, and data access events within your organization. Administrators can access the audit log by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Audit Log. Logs can be filtered by date range, event type, and user. Audit logs can be exported in CSV or JSON format for integration with your SIEM or compliance tooling. Log retention is 12 months for Professional plan customers and 24 months for Enterprise plan customers.

Security Incident Reporting
If you suspect a security incident involving your AcmeTech organization, contact the AcmeTech Security team immediately at security@acmetechsolutions.com. Include your organization name, a description of the suspected incident, and any relevant details. AcmeTech's security team will acknowledge your report within four hours and provide a status update within 24 hours. ||| Document ID: HR-002
Category: HR Policies
Title: Work From Home Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Work From Home Policy
Effective Date: March 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions recognizes that flexible workplace arrangements can enhance employee productivity, job satisfaction, and work-life balance. This Work From Home Policy governs the conditions under which employees may work remotely and establishes the standards of professional conduct, availability, and data security expected of remote employees.

Eligibility
Work from home arrangements are available to employees who have completed their probationary period and whose role has been assessed as suitable for remote work by their manager and HR Business Partner. Roles requiring on-site physical presence, client-facing duties in person, or access to secure facilities are not eligible for regular work from home arrangements. Eligibility may be reviewed and adjusted at any time based on business needs.

Standard Remote Work Arrangements
AcmeTech employees on approved work from home arrangements may work remotely up to three days per week, with a minimum of two days per week spent in the designated office location. Employees wishing to work from home on specific days must inform their manager by 9:00 AM on the day in question and ensure availability via AcmeTech's standard communication tools throughout core hours of 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM local time.

Home Office Requirements
Employees working from home are responsible for maintaining a safe, ergonomic, and distraction-free work environment. AcmeTech will provide a remote work equipment stipend of up to $500 per year to support home office setup. Employees must use AcmeTech-approved devices and must connect to the company VPN at all times when accessing internal systems remotely. Use of personal devices to access company data requires prior approval from the IT Security team.

Performance and Availability Standards
Remote employees are held to the same performance standards as office-based employees. Failure to meet performance expectations will result in the work from home arrangement being reviewed and potentially revoked. Employees on performance improvement plans are not eligible for work from home arrangements without explicit HR approval.

Compliance and Review
This policy will be reviewed annually by the People Operations team in consultation with department heads. Violations of this policy, including failure to maintain VPN connectivity or unapproved use of personal devices for company work, may result in revocation of remote work privileges and potential disciplinary action. ||| Document ID: HR-014

Employees must submit leave requests through the AcmeTech HR self-service portal with a minimum of five business days notice for absences of one to three days, and a minimum of ten business days notice for absences of four or more consecutive days. Requests are subject to manager approval, which should be granted or declined within three business days of submission. Managers are responsible for ensuring adequate team coverage during leave periods. Denial of a leave request must be accompanied by a written explanation and a proposed alternative leave window.

Leave During Notice Period
Employees who have resigned or been issued notice of termination may take approved annual leave during the notice period at the manager's discretion. In cases where leave cannot be accommodated, accrued leave balances will be paid out in the final paycheck in accordance with applicable employment law.

Policy Compliance
Failure to obtain leave approval before absence, falsification of leave records, or abuse of the leave policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Employees with concerns about leave entitlements or approvals should contact the HR department at hrteam@acmetechsolutions.com.

Title: CI/CD Deployment Process

AcmeTech Solutions CI/CD Deployment Process
Version: 3.0
Last Updated: April 2026
Owner: DevOps and Platform Engineering Teams

Overview
This document describes AcmeTech's continuous integration and continuous delivery pipeline, including pipeline stages, quality gates, deployment automation, and environment promotion procedures. A well-governed CI/CD pipeline is critical to delivering software reliably, safely, and at velocity.

Pipeline Stages
AcmeTech's CI/CD pipeline is implemented in GitHub Actions and consists of six stages. Stage 1 – Code Validation: Linting, static analysis, and security scanning using Semgrep and Snyk. Stage 2 – Unit Tests: Execution of the service's unit test suite with a minimum 80% code coverage requirement. Stage 3 – Build: Docker image build and push to the AcmeTech container registry with image signing. Stage 4 – Integration Tests: Service deployed to the CI environment and run against the integration test suite. Stage 5 – Staging Deployment: Automated deployment to the staging environment with smoke test validation. Stage 6 – Production Deployment: Gated deployment to production requiring an additional approval step for changes classified as high-risk.

Branch Strategy
AcmeTech follows a trunk-based development model. All development work is performed on short-lived feature branches created from the main branch. Feature branches must be reviewed and approved by at least one peer engineer before merging. Direct commits to the main branch are blocked. The main branch deploys automatically to the staging environment. Production deployments are triggered manually by a designated release engineer after staging validation.

Deployment Approvals
Standard production deployments require approval from the engineer's team lead or a designated release engineer. Deployments classified as high-risk based on the risk assessment rubric require additional approval from the Engineering Director. The risk assessment rubric evaluates changes based on their blast radius, rollback complexity, dependency on external systems, and changes to data schemas or API contracts.

Deployment Windows
Standard production deployments are permitted Monday through Thursday between 10 AM and 3 PM Pacific Time. Friday deployments require VP of Engineering approval. Deployments are prohibited on the day before and after a US federal holiday. Emergency deployments outside these windows can be approved by the on-call senior engineer using the emergency deployment procedure documented in the runbook.

Rollback Procedures
Every deployment must have a documented rollback plan before being approved. Automated rollback is triggered if post-deployment health checks fail within five minutes. Manual rollback can be executed through the deployment control panel. Data migrations that cannot be reversed must be flagged in the deployment plan and approved by the Engineering Director before deployment proceeds. ||| Document ID: EMP-002

Title: Production Release Checklist

AcmeTech Solutions Production Release Checklist
Version: 2.4
Last Updated: March 2026
Owner: Engineering Operations

Overview
This checklist must be completed by the release engineer and reviewed by the engineering team lead before any production deployment proceeds. The checklist ensures that all technical, operational, and communication requirements are satisfied prior to releasing changes to the production environment.

Pre-Deployment Checklist

Technical Readiness
- All automated pipeline stages have passed including linting, unit tests, security scanning, and integration tests.
- Code has been peer reviewed and approved by a minimum of one engineer.
- Database migrations, if any, have been reviewed by the Database Engineering team and marked backward compatible or flagged for coordinated cutover.
- API contract changes, if any, have been reviewed by the Platform Engineering team.
- Feature flags are configured correctly in the feature flag management system.
- The deployment has been successfully validated in the staging environment.
- Rollback procedure has been documented and tested where applicable.

Operational Readiness
- Monitoring dashboards and alerting rules have been verified to be in place.
- On-call engineer is aware of the deployment and has been briefed on what to watch for.
- Any temporary elevated alerting thresholds have been configured for the expected post-deployment settling period.
- Customer support team has been notified if the deployment involves user-visible changes.

Communication Readiness
- Internal deployment notification has been posted to the #deployments Slack channel.
- Status page has been updated if the deployment involves planned maintenance or expected brief disruption.
- Customer communications, if required, have been reviewed and are ready to send.

Deployment Execution
- Deployment initiated by designated release engineer.
- Health checks monitored for minimum 10 minutes post-deployment.
- Key metrics reviewed in Grafana dashboard for anomalies.
- Rollback procedure confirmed not needed.
- Post-deployment summary posted to #deployments channel.

Post-Deployment Sign-Off
Release Engineer sign-off required. Team Lead sign-off required. Engineering Director sign-off required for high-risk deployments.

question: What level of approval is required for a $75,000 purchase, and would it need Board or Audit Committee sign-off?
answer:
A $75,000 purchase falls under **Tier 4** of the Procurement Approval Matrix, requiring **CFO approval**. Since the amount is above $50,000 but below $250,000, **Board or Audit Committee approval is not required**.

Title: Procurement Approval Matrix

AcmeTech Solutions Procurement Approval Matrix
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions requires appropriate authorization for all procurement activities to ensure that expenditures are aligned with approved budgets, support legitimate business needs, and are subject to adequate competitive evaluation. This Procurement Approval Matrix defines the authorization levels required for all purchase categories and amounts.

Approval Tiers
Tier 1: Purchases up to $1,000 may be approved by the employee's direct manager. Tier 2: Purchases between $1,001 and $10,000 require approval from the department head or Director. Tier 3: Purchases between $10,001 and $50,000 require approval from the VP of the relevant function and the Finance Director. Tier 4: Purchases above $50,000 require CFO approval and, for amounts above $250,000, Board or Audit Committee approval is required.

Vendor Selection Requirements
All new vendor relationships above $5,000 in annual spend require a minimum of three competitive quotes unless a sole-source justification is approved by the Finance Director. Preferred vendor agreements must be reviewed and renewed annually. Vendors must complete AcmeTech's standard vendor onboarding documentation, including proof of insurance, tax forms, and anti-corruption attestation, before any purchase order can be issued.

Purchase Order Requirements
All purchases above $2,500 require a formal purchase order issued through AcmeTech's procurement system before any commitment is made to a vendor. Purchase orders must reference an approved budget line item. Verbal commitments to vendors without a purchase order are not binding on AcmeTech and will not be honored for payment. Employees who commit to purchases without proper authorization are personally responsible for those costs.

IT and Software Procurement
All technology purchases, including software licenses, cloud services, and hardware, must be reviewed and approved by the IT Security and Architecture teams before budget and procurement approval is sought. Shadow IT purchases are strictly prohibited. The IT team maintains a list of approved software vendors and security-reviewed tools accessible on the internal knowledge base.

Sole-Source and Emergency Procurement
In cases where competitive bidding is not practical due to urgency or unique vendor capabilities, a sole-source justification must be submitted to the Finance Director within 24 hours of the purchase commitment. The justification must include the reason competitive bids were not obtained and documentation of the business urgency. Emergency procurements above $50,000 require CFO notification within the same business day. ||| Document ID: FIN-007
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Audit Compliance Procedures

AcmeTech Solutions Audit Compliance Procedures
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Internal Audit

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions is committed to maintaining financial records and internal controls that meet the highest standards of accuracy, completeness, and compliance. This document outlines the procedures for managing both internal and external audits, including preparation, coordination, response, and remediation activities.

Annual External Audit
AcmeTech undergoes an annual financial statement audit conducted by an independent external audit firm. The audit covers all financial statements for the calendar year and is completed in accordance with Generally Accepted Auditing Standards. The Finance team must have all year-end close activities completed and financial statements prepared no later than February 28. Audit fieldwork typically begins in March, with the final audit opinion issued no later than April 30.

Internal Audit Program
AcmeTech maintains an internal audit function that conducts risk-based audits of key financial and operational processes throughout the year. The annual internal audit plan is approved by the Audit Committee and covers areas including payroll controls, expense management, procurement compliance, revenue recognition, and IT general controls. Internal audit findings are reported to the CFO and Audit Committee on a quarterly basis.

Audit Preparation
Department heads are responsible for ensuring their teams maintain complete, accurate, and well-organized documentation for all financial transactions throughout the year. Finance Business Partners will provide audit preparation checklists to each department four weeks before the scheduled audit fieldwork commences. Employees must respond to auditor requests for documentation within three business days.

Control Remediation
Any control deficiencies identified through internal or external audit are documented in a remediation tracker maintained by the Finance team. Each finding is assigned an owner, a remediation plan, and a target completion date. Remediation progress is reviewed monthly by the CFO. Critical findings must be remediated within 60 days. High-priority findings must be remediated within 90 days.

Fraud Prevention and Detection
AcmeTech maintains a zero-tolerance policy for financial fraud. The Finance team is responsible for implementing preventive controls including segregation of duties, dual approval requirements for disbursements over $10,000, and regular reconciliation of all balance sheet accounts. Employees who suspect financial fraud must report it immediately through the Ethics Hotline. Retaliation against fraud reporters is strictly prohibited. ||| Document ID: FIN-010
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Vendor Payment Process

AcmeTech Solutions Vendor Payment Process
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Accounts Payable

Policy Statement
This document describes the standard process for processing and disbursing vendor payments at AcmeTech Solutions. All vendor payments must adhere to this process to ensure accuracy, proper authorization, and compliance with AcmeTech's financial controls.

Invoice Receipt and Validation
Vendor invoices must be sent directly to AcmeTech's designated accounts payable email address or submitted through the vendor portal. Invoices submitted to individual employees will be redirected to accounts payable; however, this practice may cause payment delays. Upon receipt, the Accounts Payable team validates each invoice against the corresponding purchase order, confirming vendor name, invoice amount, line items, and delivery confirmation where applicable.

Three-Way Match
AcmeTech requires a three-way match for all invoices above $1,000: the invoice is matched against the purchase order and the goods receipt or service confirmation. Invoices that fail the three-way match will be placed on hold and the originating department will be notified within two business days to resolve the discrepancy. Payment will not be processed until the three-way match is confirmed.

Payment Terms and Scheduling
Standard payment terms with vendors are Net 30 from the date of invoice receipt. Early payment discount terms will be honored where they represent a favorable return on capital, subject to Finance Director approval. Accounts Payable generates a weekly payment run every Thursday, covering all invoices approved for payment during the prior week. Urgent payment requests outside the standard payment run require VP-level approval.

Payment Methods
AcmeTech disburses vendor payments primarily via ACH bank transfer. Wire transfers are used for international vendors or in cases where ACH is not available. Checks are issued only in exceptional circumstances and require Finance Director approval. New vendor banking details must be verified through a dual-authentication process before the first payment is made to prevent fraudulent redirection of funds.

Payment Disputes and Credits
Vendors with disputed invoices must contact the accounts payable team in writing within 30 days of the invoice date. AcmeTech will investigate and respond within 10 business days. Approved credit memos from vendors will be applied to future invoices from the same vendor. Refund requests from vendors are processed through the same payment channels as standard disbursements. ||| Document ID: FIN-011
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Revenue Recognition Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Revenue Recognition Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions recognizes revenue in accordance with ASC 606, Revenue from Contracts with Customers. This policy ensures that revenue is recorded accurately, consistently, and in compliance with applicable accounting standards. All revenue-related transactions must be reviewed by Finance before recognition.

Five-Step Model
AcmeTech applies the ASC 606 five-step model to all customer contracts. Step 1: Identify the contract with the customer. Step 2: Identify the performance obligations in the contract. Step 3: Determine the transaction price. Step 4: Allocate the transaction price to each performance obligation. Step 5: Recognize revenue when or as each performance obligation is satisfied.

SaaS Subscription Revenue
Subscription revenue is recognized ratably over the contract term, beginning on the date the customer's access to the platform is provisioned. Annual and multi-year subscription payments received in advance are recorded as deferred revenue and recognized proportionally on a monthly basis. Any discounts or variable consideration in subscription contracts must be evaluated and documented by Finance before contract execution.

Professional Services Revenue
Revenue from implementation and consulting services is recognized as services are delivered, based on the percentage of completion method. Project milestones used for revenue recognition must be documented in the statement of work and approved by Finance prior to contract signing. Revenue from time-and-materials engagements is recognized monthly based on actual hours delivered.

Contract Modifications
Any changes to existing customer contracts that affect the scope, price, or term must be reviewed by Finance before execution. Modifications may require revenue to be reallocated among performance obligations or may be treated as a separate contract depending on the nature of the change. Sales and Legal must engage Finance on all material contract modifications.

question: How quickly must a High severity (CVSS 7.0-8.9) vulnerability be remediated?
answer:
High severity vulnerabilities (CVSS 7.0–8.9) must be remediated within **7 days**.
